name: Letta (Stateful AI Agents)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Letta AI; Letta Cloud available with paid tiers

auth.notes: Letta Cloud: API key; self-hosted: no auth by default

performance.notes: LLM call latency dominates; memory management adds overhead; stateful context management

agent_readiness.notes: Stateful agent framework formerly known as MemGPT. Agents with persistent memory across conversations. Memory types: core (in-context), archival (vector), recall (conversation history). Self-editing memory via tool calls. REST API server with Python SDK. Supports custom tools (Python functions). Multi-agent deployment. ADE (Agent Development Environment) UI. Memory management mimics OS virtual memory for infinite context. LLM-agnostic: OpenAI, Anthropic, local models.

gotchas: More complex setup than stateless agent frameworks
Memory operations add latency (archival search via embeddings)
Early-stage product (rebranded from MemGPT) with evolving API
best_when: Long-running AI assistants needing persistent memory
Personal AI companions or customer service agents across sessions
Applications where context preservation across conversations is critical
Research on stateful agent memory management
summary: Letta (formerly MemGPT) is a stateful agent framework with persistent memory across conversations. Three memory layers: core (in-context), archival (vector search), and recall (conversation history). Agents self-manage memory via tool calls. REST API with Python SDK. LLM-agnostic. ADE visual environment for agent development. Best for AI assistants requiring long-term memory and cross-session context persistence.
